title: Brownfield Access Review Security Design
prompt: Read `./access-review-modernization-architecture.md` and `./access-review-modernization-api-contract.md`. Work only from those files. Do not ask follow-up questions. If you need assumptions, state them briefly and continue. Produce the threat-model artifact for this brownfield modernization slice. Identify the high-value assets, trust boundaries, abuse paths, and concrete controls. Keep legacy coexistence, tenant isolation, evidence integrity, and fail-closed unsupported-flow handling explicit.

assertions.name: identifies-assets-and-trust-boundaries
assertions.description: Sensitive assets and trust transitions are explicit.

assertions.name: enumerates-real-abuse-paths
assertions.description: Tenant isolation, replay, logging leakage, and coexistence weaknesses are considered.

assertions.name: assigns-concrete-controls
assertions.description: Controls are mapped to concrete system boundaries.

assertions.name: keeps-brownfield-risk-visible
assertions.description: Legacy and compatibility exposure remain explicit.

assertions.name: prepares-audit-handoff
assertions.description: The resulting threat model can feed security audit directly.
